seo.title: AI Medical Record Review for Law Firms | Dodonai
seo.description: Upload 500 pages of medical records. Get a chronology with page citations in 15 minutes. SOC 2 certified. HIPAA compliant. Try free.

hero.headline: AI Medical Record Review — Analyze Patient Records Faster
hero.subheadline: Medical record review is the analysis step — reading patient records and extracting the clinical events that matter to your case. Dodonai's AI reads the records for your team, flagging diagnoses, treatments, inconsistencies, and timeline gaps so attorneys review conclusions, not raw documents.

sections.heading: What Medical Record Review Actually Means
paragraphs: Medical record review is the analysis step in any case involving patient records — physician notes, diagnostic results, imaging reports, treatment history, IME findings. The job is to read the records, identify what's clinically relevant, flag inconsistencies, and surface the moments that affect case strategy. For attorneys, that means reading thousands of pages before you can build a {{link:/ai-medical-record-summaries-chronologies/|medical chronology}}, evaluate damages, or prepare for deposition.

Manual review hasn't changed much in decades: open the file, read every page, take notes. For a 500-page record set, that's a full day of paralegal time. Multiply that across 20 active cases and your team is buried in paperwork — and still has to build the chronology afterward.

AI medical record review changes the equation. Dodonai ingests the full record set, identifies key clinical events, flags inconsistencies and treatment gaps, and produces structured output with page citations back to the source. Your team reviews the AI's findings instead of reading every page line by line. For law firms handling personal injury, medical malpractice, mass tort, workers' comp, or {{link:/practice-areas/disability-benefits/|disability benefits cases}}, the result is faster case evaluation, more consistent analysis across the firm, and lower cost per case.

sections.heading: What the AI Does During Review

cards.title: Clinical Event Extraction
cards.description: The AI reads every page and extracts diagnoses, procedures, medications, provider visits, lab results, and imaging findings — with a page citation for every extracted event so any finding can be verified against the source record in seconds.

cards.title: Inconsistency and Gap Flagging
cards.description: Surfaces inconsistencies across providers, gaps in treatment, contradictions between IME findings and treating physician notes, and prior conditions that could affect causation analysis. The signals attorneys would otherwise hunt for manually.

cards.title: Narrative Analysis Summary
cards.description: A readable analytical summary of the patient's clinical picture — key diagnoses, course of treatment, response to therapy, and the threads that connect them. Built for case evaluation and {{link:/use-cases/expert-witness-preparation/|expert witness preparation}}, not just data extraction.

cards.title: OCR for Scanned Records
cards.description: Scanned PDFs, handwritten notes, and faxed documents are no problem. Dodonai's {{link:/ai-pdf-ocr/|AI-powered OCR}} extracts text accurately so nothing gets missed during review.

cards.title: HIPAA-Compliant Processing
cards.description: Patient data is encrypted with AES-256 on SOC 2 certified infrastructure. BAAs signed before your first upload. Records never enter a training pipeline.

cards.title: Bulk Record Processing
cards.description: Whether it's a single plaintiff case or thousands of claimants in a mass tort, upload and process multiple record sets in parallel. Dodonai scales to your volume without sacrificing analysis quality.

items.title: Upload Your Medical Records
items.description: Upload the full record set to Dodonai's secure platform. Accepts PDFs of any size, including scanned and handwritten documents. Specify key topics, providers, or diagnoses to focus the analysis.

items.title: AI Reads and Analyzes the Records
items.description: Dodonai reads every page, extracts the clinically relevant events, and flags inconsistencies and treatment gaps. Every extracted event includes a page citation back to the source so any finding is verifiable in one click.

items.title: Review the AI's Findings, Not Every Page
items.description: Open the analysis in the side-by-side viewer. Sort and filter by date, provider, or event type. Make adjustments to the analysis, then export in your preferred format — or hand the analyzed record set off to chronology generation.

sections.heading: AI vs. Manual Medical Record Review
rows: Read and analyze 500+ pages in under 15 minutes
Flag inconsistencies and treatment gaps automatically
Scale to thousands of cases simultaneously
Source-page citations for every extracted event

sections.heading: From Medical Record Review to Chronology
paragraphs: Review is the input. The chronology is the output. Once Dodonai has read and analyzed the records, it automatically organizes the extracted events into a complete medical chronology — a structured, date-ordered timeline your team can use in depositions, mediation, demand letters, and trial. The analysis and the chronology come from a single upload; you don't run two tools.

If you want to see the chronology output specifically — formats, examples, and how attorneys use it — {{link:/ai-medical-record-summaries-chronologies/|generate a full medical chronology}} is the dedicated page for that. If you're comparing chronology software vendors, see our {{link:/compare/best-medical-chronology-software/|comparison of medical chronology software options}}.

faq.question: How accurate is AI medical record review compared to manual review?
faq.answer: Dodonai extracts clinical events with high precision across provider charting styles. Every extracted event includes a page citation so reviewers can verify any finding against the original record. The structured output reduces the oversight errors that occur when paralegals process large volumes manually under time pressure.

faq.question: What types of medical records can Dodonai analyze?
faq.answer: Hospital discharge summaries, SOAP notes, operative reports, lab panels, imaging studies, therapy notes, pharmacy records, IME reports, and correspondence. The {{link:/ai-pdf-ocr/|AI OCR engine}} extracts text from low-quality scans, so scanned faxes and handwritten notes get analyzed alongside digital records.

faq.question: Is the platform HIPAA compliant?
faq.answer: Encrypted from upload to deletion with AES-256. SOC 2 certified, HIPAA compliant, with signed BAAs on every account. Your records stay out of training pipelines, and your firm controls the retention schedule.

faq.question: How long does AI medical record review take?
faq.answer: Most record sets process in minutes regardless of length. A 500-page record set that would take a paralegal a full day to review manually is analyzed in under 15 minutes. The output is ready to review immediately — no overnight batch wait.

faq.question: How is medical record review different from generating a medical chronology?
faq.answer: Review is the analysis step — reading the records and extracting relevant clinical events. The chronology is the structured output — those events arranged into a date-ordered timeline. Dodonai handles both from a single upload: see the {{link:/ai-medical-record-summaries-chronologies/|chronology page}} for output formats and examples.

faq.question: What practice areas benefit most from AI medical record review?
faq.answer: Any litigation involving medical records: {{link:/practice-areas/personal-injury/|personal injury}}, medical malpractice, mass torts, workers' comp, and {{link:/practice-areas/disability-benefits/|disability benefits medical records}}. Insurance companies and {{link:/industries/ime-companies/|IME companies}} also rely on Dodonai for high-volume processing.
